breach_claims: The Agreement caps each party’s aggregate liability across all SOWs at a maximum of $250,000 in total during the entire Term of the Agreement.

Because “Authorized Users” are defined in Section 1.10 as Customer’s employees and contractors, the Agreement expressly prohibits Customer from designating agents or other third‑party representatives who are not employees or contractors as Authorized Users of the Service.

Under this Agreement, a Party’s independent contractors are included within the definition of its “Representatives,” allowing the Receiving Party to share Confidential Information with such contractors so long as they are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as those in the Agreement.
Provider guarantees a Monthly Uptime Percentage of at least 99.5% for the Service.

Under this DPA, any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person that the Processor handles in connection with its services to the Controller is treated as “Personal Data,” regardless of whether it is Processed on behalf of the Controller under the Agreement.

Under Section 10, any liability arising from Service Provider’s gross negligence or willful misconduct is not subject to the monetary cap in Section 10.2 and may exceed the total Fees paid or payable under the applicable SOW.

Under this DPA, if the Controller objects in writing within ten days to a newly proposed Sub-Processor, the Processor is expressly prohibited from engaging that Sub-Processor to Process Personal Data on behalf of the Controller.
If the Provider breaches Section 5 (Data Ownership), the limitation of liability cap in Section 8.1 does not apply and the Provider’s liability is unlimited for such breach.

Under Section 3.1, if the Controller timely objects to a proposed new Sub-Processor, the Processor is expressly prohibited from engaging that Sub-Processor to Process Personal Data on behalf of the Controller.
Under this Agreement, claims arising from gross negligence or willful misconduct are expressly exempt from the monetary cap on liability set forth in Section 10.2.
